title: install --update overwrites a locally-modified schema without consulting the checkStatus drift classification
description: The update path overwrites installed schemas (resyncSchema) without first consulting the checkStatus drift detector, which already classifies each installed schema as in-sync / catalog-ahead / locally-modified / both-diverged. A locally-modified (user-edited) schema is clobbered with no detection or warning, even though the classification that would flag it is computable and shipped.

evidence.reference: resyncSchema overwrites the installed schema (verbatim same-version, migrate-or-refuse on bump) without reading the checkStatus locally-modified classification
checkStatus computes the drift classification but the --update write path never calls it before clobbering

impact: User schema edits are destroyed silently on update; the drift signal needed to prevent it exists but is not consulted in the write path.
proposed_resolution: The update path consults checkStatus before re-syncing each schema and refuses-or-merges (per FGAP-046) any locally-modified schema instead of overwriting it; delivered within the unified update command (FEAT-006).
